justification: scoring parse error: claude scoring exited non-zero:

trials.justification: scoring parse error: claude scoring exited non-zero:

The agent failed to produce a handoff brief at all. While it correctly noted some ambiguity around the 'appealed' state visibility in the low_confidence_areas and assumptions sections, it did not explicitly identify the core AC3 ambiguity — that 'accepted' outcome is undefined. It did not flag the mismatch between MJ's state machine (appealed/restored states) and Bird's acceptance criteria. There is no escalation to Bird or Coach K about the AC3 ambiguity; the only escalation is about missing Pippen input, which is not the critical issue. The non-ambiguous parts of the brief (feed filtering, author visibility, 7-day window) were not included in an actionable handoff. The agent effectively blocked the entire brief due to missing Pippen input rather than delivering the unambiguous content.

observations.text: AC3 ambiguity ('accepted' outcome undefined) not explicitly identified or escalated
No escalation to Bird or Coach K about the appeal outcome definition gap
MJ's appealed/restored state machine mismatch with Bird's ACs not flagged as an escalation
Entire brief blocked — no actionable handoff with unambiguous content (feed filtering, author view, 7-day window)
The sole escalation is about missing Pippen input, which is tangential to the core ambiguity problem
Assumptions section hints at the appealed-state visibility gap, showing partial awareness of the issue
Low confidence areas mention appeal deadline enforcement edge cases

trials.justification: The agent missed every core rubric criterion. (1) AC3 ambiguity ('accepted' outcome undefined) is never explicitly identified — the low_confidence_areas mention 'appeal state transition edge cases' tangentially but never call out that AC3's 'is accepted' lacks a defined outcome. (2) MJ's state machine mismatch with Bird's ACs is not flagged — worse, high_confidence_areas claims MJ's state model 'aligns cleanly with Bird's acceptance criteria,' which is the opposite of what should be flagged (Bird never defines 'appealed' or 'restored' states). (3) No escalation to Coach K or Bird for clarification on the appeal outcome — the only escalation is about missing Pippen input, which is irrelevant to the scenario's core problem. (4) No actionable handoff brief is produced at all — neither the unambiguous parts (feed filtering, author visibility, 7-day window) nor the ambiguous parts are structured for Shaq. (5) The entire output is blocked on a missing Pippen review rather than isolating AC3 as the ambiguous element and forwarding the rest.

observations.text: AC3 ambiguity never explicitly identified — 'accepted' outcome undefined is the central issue and it is missed
High confidence area claims MJ's state model 'aligns cleanly' with Bird's ACs — this is factually wrong and a rubric failure mode (treating 'appealed'/'restored' states as self-explanatory)
Escalation is about missing Pippen input, not about the AC3/state-machine mismatch that the scenario requires
No handoff brief produced — unambiguous content (feed filtering, author view, 7-day window) is not forwarded to Shaq
Entire output effectively blocked on Pippen's absence, violating the rubric criterion that unambiguous parts should remain actionable
Low confidence area mentions appeal state transition edge cases and 7-day window timezone ambiguity, showing some awareness of gaps — but these are too vague and miss the specific AC3 problem
